name: blockenberg/comparison-guide
title: Comparison Guide
description: A structured X vs Y comparison guide with criteria scoring, winner badges, and a verdict. Perfect for product, software, and strategy comparisons.

guideTitle.default: React vs Vue: Which Framework Should You Choose in 2026?

intro.default: Both React and Vue are excellent choices for modern web development. This guide breaks down the key decision criteria so you can choose the right tool for your project.

descA.default: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Meta.

descB.default: The progressive JavaScript framework for building web interfaces, created by Evan You.

default.name: Learning Curve
default.notes: Vue's template syntax feels more natural to developers coming from HTML/CSS backgrounds. React's JSX takes more adjustment.

default.name: Ecosystem & Libraries
default.notes: React has the largest ecosystem by far, including React Native for mobile, Next.js for SSR, and thousands of UI component libraries.

default.name: Performance
default.notes: Both frameworks deliver excellent performance with virtual DOM diffing. The difference is negligible for most real-world applications.

default.name: Job Market
default.notes: React dominates job postings globally, making it the safer career investment for front-end developers.

default.name: Official Tooling
default.notes: Vue's official tooling (Vite integration, Pinia, Vue Router) is more cohesive and better documented than React's fragmented ecosystem.

default.name: Corporate Backing
default.notes: React is backed by Meta. Vue is community-driven — an advantage for independence but a risk for long-term funding.

verdictSummary.default: React is the better choice for teams prioritizing employability, ecosystem depth, and mobile development via React Native. Choose Vue if you value a gentler learning curve, tighter official tooling, or are building smaller to mid-size SPAs.
